sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Level 2 has a mirrored layout compared to Level 1:
- Dark gray (G) is now on the RIGHT side (cols 8-63)
- White (W) area is on the LEFT (cols 0-51 above, cols 0-11 in middle, cols 0-7 below)
- Black (B) horizontal barriers with gaps
- Blue (b) targets on the LEFT side (cols 0-3)
- Player (c) at (52,10)-(57,13)
- Light green (N) strip at (52,8)-(57,9) - this is like the yellow strip in Level 1
- Another green gap at (40,28)-(43,29) in the black barrier
So the layout is mirrored. The player is on the right side of the white area, and needs to move LEFT (toward the blue targets) to align with the green gap in the barrier.
In Level 1, clicking the upper blue moved right and lower blue moved left. Here, the blue targets are on the left side, so the mechanic might be:
- Clicking upper blue moves player LEFT
- Clicking lower blue moves player RIGHT
Or it could be the same: upper=right, lower=left. Let me check the blue target positions:
- Blue targets at cols 0-3, rows: 16-19, 24-27, 36-39, 44-47
- Player at cols 10-13, rows 52-57
- Green gap at cols 28-29, rows 40-43
Wait, there are 4 blue targets now! In Level 1 there were only 2. Let me think about this.
The player is at cols 10-13. The green gap is at cols 28-29. To align with the gap, the player needs to move RIGHT by about 16 cells (4 steps of 4 cells each).
In Level 1, clicking the upper blue target moved the player RIGHT. So maybe here, clicking the upper blue target also moves RIGHT. Let me try clicking the uppermost blue target (rows 16-19, cols 0-3).
Actually, let me reconsider. In Level 1:
- Upper blue (rows 24-27) → RIGHT
- Lower blue (rows 32-35) → LEFT
Here we have 4 blue targets at rows 16-19, 24-27, 36-39, 44-47. Maybe different targets move in different directions or by different amounts.
Let me try clicking the top blue target to see what happens.
